raw_response: {"rating": -4.5, "lean": "Left", "explanation": "This is a news report with strong and consistent left-leaning framing throughout the author's own prose. The piece presents abortion bans as a direct cause of preventable deaths, relies almost exclusively on sources and framing that support that narrative, and characterizes anti-abortion opposition in dismissive terms. The accumulation of directional editorial choices across multiple dimensions warrants a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The headline — 'The deaths from abortion bans you won't hear about' — is the author's own framing and presupposes a causal relationship between abortion bans and deaths while also implying deliberate suppression of information, both politically charged claims embedded before the article begins; (2) The author describes anti-abortion activists as going 'into victim-blaming mode' in their own prose, a loaded characterization that dismisses opposition rather than describing it neutrally; (3) The embedded related-article link headline — 'A leading abortion provider on why restrictive laws are taking us back to the Dark Ages' — is an editorial choice by the author or editor that frames abortion restrictions in maximally negative terms; (4) The author's own framing of Candi Miller's death — 'she died in bed, afraid and in pain' — is emotionally charged narrative language that goes well beyond neutral news reporting and is designed to maximize reader sympathy; (5) Source selection is entirely one-directional: all named experts and institutional sources (UCSF ANSIRH, Jessica Valenti, Carole Joffe) oppose abortion bans; no source representing the opposing legal or medical perspective appears anywhere in the piece; (6) Valenti's book is described neutrally as a source without noting her identity as an abortion rights activist, obscuring the ideological nature of that source from readers; (7) The phrase 'so-called exceptions' in the author's own prose dismisses the stated intent of abortion ban legislation as fraudulent or ineffective without attribution to any source; (8) The Salon subscription call-out embedded in the article reads 'support Salon's progressive journalism,' which is an editorial self-identification confirming the ideological orientation of the piece's framing choices."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report with strong and consistent left-leaning framing throughout the author's own prose. The piece presents abortion bans as a direct cause of preventable deaths, relies almost exclusively on sources and framing that support that narrative, and characterizes anti-abortion opposition in dismissive terms. The accumulation of directional editorial choices across multiple dimensions warrants a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The headline — 'The deaths from abortion bans you won't hear about' — is the author's own framing and presupposes a causal relationship between abortion bans and deaths while also implying deliberate suppression of information, both politically charged claims embedded before the article begins; (2) The author describes anti-abortion activists as going 'into victim-blaming mode' in their own prose, a loaded characterization that dismisses opposition rather than describing it neutrally; (3) The embedded related-article link headline — 'A leading abortion provider on why restrictive laws are taking us back to the Dark Ages' — is an editorial choice by the author or editor that frames abortion restrictions in maximally negative terms; (4) The author's own framing of Candi Miller's death — 'she died in bed, afraid and in pain' — is emotionally charged narrative language that goes well beyond neutral news reporting and is designed to maximize reader sympathy; (5) Source selection is entirely one-directional: all named experts and institutional sources (UCSF ANSIRH, Jessica Valenti, Carole Joffe) oppose abortion bans; no source representing the opposing legal or medical perspective appears anywhere in the piece; (6) Valenti's book is described neutrally as a source without noting her identity as an abortion rights activist, obscuring the ideological nature of that source from readers; (7) The phrase 'so-called exceptions' in the author's own prose dismisses the stated intent of abortion ban legislation as fraudulent or ineffective without attribution to any source; (8) The Salon subscription call-out embedded in the article reads 'support Salon's progressive journalism,' which is an editorial self-identification confirming the ideological orientation of the piece's framing choices.

explanation: This is a news report with strong and consistent left-leaning framing throughout the author's own prose. The piece presents abortion bans as a direct cause of preventable deaths, relies almost exclusively on sources and framing that support that narrative, and characterizes anti-abortion opposition in dismissive terms. The accumulation of directional editorial choices across multiple dimensions warrants a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The headline — 'The deaths from abortion bans you won't hear about' — is the author's own framing and presupposes a causal relationship between abortion bans and deaths while also implying deliberate suppression of information, both politically charged claims embedded before the article begins; (2) The author describes anti-abortion activists as going 'into victim-blaming mode' in their own prose, a loaded characterization that dismisses opposition rather than describing it neutrally; (3) The embedded related-article link headline — 'A leading abortion provider on why restrictive laws are taking us back to the Dark Ages' — is an editorial choice by the author or editor that frames abortion restrictions in maximally negative terms; (4) The author's own framing of Candi Miller's death — 'she died in bed, afraid and in pain' — is emotionally charged narrative language that goes well beyond neutral news reporting and is designed to maximize reader sympathy; (5) Source selection is entirely one-directional: all named experts and institutional sources (UCSF ANSIRH, Jessica Valenti, Carole Joffe) oppose abortion bans; no source representing the opposing legal or medical perspective appears anywhere in the piece; (6) Valenti's book is described neutrally as a source without noting her identity as an abortion rights activist, obscuring the ideological nature of that source from readers; (7) The phrase 'so-called exceptions' in the author's own prose dismisses the stated intent of abortion ban legislation as fraudulent or ineffective without attribution to any source; (8) The Salon subscription call-out embedded in the article reads 'support Salon's progressive journalism,' which is an editorial self-identification confirming the ideological orientation of the piece's framing choices.
